overall_assessment: 7 findings reviewed across TSA and RA artifacts (TSA-001, TSA-002, TSA-003, RA-001, RA-002, RA-003, RA-004). Independent recomputation against smoke-test.csv reproduces every numeric claim within rounding tolerance. Grades held: 2 at A (RA-003, RA-004), 4 at B (TSA-001, TSA-002, RA-001, RA-002), 1 at C (TSA-003). Zero findings filtered. One minor methodological discrepancy on TSA-001's reported CI (upstream narrower than independently computed). Cross-cutting: 4-week window caps all temporal findings below grade A unless effect is unambiguous; RA-003 and RA-004 qualify because they are cross-sectional and structural. Guardrail pairing check returned missing_data for findings without a data-derivable counter-metric because no domain context document was provided; where pairings could be constructed from the data, they were performed and surfaced a dual-concern at A001 (fill_rate decline + week-4 volume dip).

discrepancy_details.explanation: Upstream CI half-width implies a critical value near 2.0; correct t-critical at df=2 is 4.303, giving the wider interval the Validator computed. Point estimate (-0.00750), p-value (0.0131), and R² (0.974) reproduce exactly. The finding still surfaces; the corrected CI is added to required_caveats.

reviewed_findings.claim: A003/SKU003 median instock 0.663 vs rest 0.931; MWU U=384, p=0.000377; complete distributional non-overlap (0.641–0.739 vs 0.881–0.989); persistent across all 4 weeks.

layer1_statistical_rigor.notes: Mann-Whitney U appropriate for left-skewed heavy-tail metric per Profiler. n_outlier=4 small but complete non-overlap makes the test conservative. Effect r=0.34 (Validator) vs 0.36 (upstream) — both medium; minor formula variant explains gap.

layer3_guardrail_pairing.result: fill_rate at A003/SKU003 is 0.940–0.976 (median 0.952), identical to population median (0.952). No supply-side trade-off — issue is shelf-side / replenishment / planogram, not DC throughput.

layer4_domain_plausibility.notes: Pattern structurally consistent with a known operational failure mode (distribution gap, persistent stockout). Severity in business terms not assessable without domain context.

simpsons_check.notes: Finding already at entity level (A003/SKU003), not an aggregate claim.

reviewed_findings.justification: All four layers pass. Independent recomputation matches exactly. Guardrail check shows fill_rate healthy at the affected entity — consistent with the finding's framing. Zero distributional overlap. Grade A correct.
required_caveats: Onset date unknown — present in earliest observable week; may have persisted prior to dataset window.
Severity (whether 0.66 vs target is moderate or severe) cannot be quantified without a domain context document specifying instock target.

reviewed_findings.claim: Volume distributions differ structurally across accounts. Kruskal-Wallis H=90.23, p≈0. Medians: A001=796.5, A002=1317, A003=429.5, A004=256, A005=2441.

layer1_statistical_rigor.notes: KW is correct non-parametric multi-group test for right-skewed volume. n=97 adequate; null records correctly excluded.

layer2_independent_recomputation.validator_value: H=90.2266, p=1.18e-18; all 5 account medians match exactly

layer3_guardrail_pairing.notes: Methodological/structural finding, not a performance claim. No counter-metric applies.

layer4_domain_plausibility.notes: ~10x volume range across accounts and a flagship account (A005) at 46.7% of total is plausible CPG structure.

simpsons_check.notes: Finding itself is the Simpson's warning.

reviewed_findings.justification: Layers 1, 2, 4 pass cleanly. Layer 3 not applicable. H and all 5 medians reproduce exactly. Grade A correct.
required_caveats: Methodological finding: warrants account-level stratification in all volume analyses but is not itself actionable by an account manager.

reviewed_findings.claim: Monotone fill_rate decline at A001 across 4 weeks (0.951 → 0.928); slope=-0.0075/wk, p=0.0131, R²=0.974; SKU004 primary contributor (slope=-0.0153, p=0.022).

layer1_statistical_rigor.notes: OLS on weekly medians is only computable temporal statistic at n=4. fill_rate approximately normal per Profiler; OLS appropriate. Upstream correctly flagged inflated R² at n=4 and treated p-value as the more conservative evidence.

layer2_independent_recomputation.notes: Point estimate, p, R², SKU decomposition reproduce exactly. Upstream CI too narrow (likely used z-critical instead of t at df=2). Correct CI is wider but still entirely below zero.

layer3_guardrail_pairing.result: A001 week-4 volume = 748 vs trailing-3 pooled median 858 (-12.8%). Not a trade-off — both fill_rate and volume weakening simultaneously at the same account. Strengthens, not contradicts, the finding.

layer4_domain_plausibility.notes: Magnitude (~2.4% over 4 weeks) consistent with operational issues. Absolute level (0.928) cannot be benchmarked without domain target.

simpsons_check.notes: SKU-level decomposition shows SKU004 primary driver, SKU001 directionally consistent, SKU002/SKU003 diverge. Concentration, not reversal — honestly described upstream.

reviewed_findings.justification: Layer 1 passes. Layer 2 partial — CI miscalculation but point estimate and significance reproduce. Layer 3 reveals dual concern at A001 (volume also down). Layer 4 plausible. CI issue is methodological; signal direction and significance hold. Grade B correct — held below A by n=4, no seasonal adjustment, and CI precision issue.
required_caveats: Corrected 95% CI for the weekly slope is [-0.0112, -0.0038]; upstream's narrower interval used an incorrect critical value at df=2. Direction and significance unchanged.
Signal rests on 4 observations; a single non-conforming week-5 observation could falsify the trend.
No prior-year or extended baseline available; cannot distinguish structural from seasonal decline.
Dual concern: A001 week-4 volume is also -12.8% below trailing baseline. Investigate A001 holistically, not fill_rate in isolation.

reviewed_findings.claim: Persistent structural underperformance across all 4 weeks; values 0.641, 0.739, 0.669, 0.656; mean 0.676; gap vs population -27.1%; within-window slope not significant (p=0.926).

layer1_statistical_rigor.notes: Resistant statistics and within-window slope appropriate. Substantively overlaps with RA-003.

layer3_guardrail_pairing.notes: Per RA-003 — fill_rate at A003/SKU003 normal.

reviewed_findings.justification: All values reproduce exactly. Substantively identical to RA-003. Communication Agent should consolidate.
required_caveats: Substantively identical to RA-003; render as a single action card.
Onset date unknown — predates observable window.

reviewed_findings.claim: A001 week-4 volume -12.8% vs trailing 3-wk pooled median (748 vs 858); aggregate-level temporal trend not significant (p=0.913). Surfaced as supporting context, not standalone signal.

layer1_statistical_rigor.notes: Upstream explicitly states the trend is NOT significant and frames week-4 dip as candidate. Honest framing.

layer2_independent_recomputation.validator_value: A001 trailing-3 pooled median=858, week-4 median=748, pct=-12.82%; aggregate slope -6.5/wk, p=0.913, R²=0.008

layer3_guardrail_pairing.result: A001 week-4 fill_rate=0.928 (down from 0.951); not a trade-off — dual concern.

layer4_domain_plausibility.notes: Single-week 13% dip at one account is within normal short-window variability; can be noise or early demand signal.

simpsons_check.notes: Aggregate week-4 -13.9% decomposes to A001 -12.8% with all others flat-to-up; upstream stratified honestly.

reviewed_findings.justification: Grade C is honest — upstream avoided promoting a non-significant signal. Should appear in descriptive summary, not as an action card. Value is purely as triangulating context for TSA-001.
required_caveats: Volume trend NOT statistically significant (p=0.913). Treat only as supporting context for TSA-001.
Single-week dip in a 4-week series indistinguishable from noise on its own.

reviewed_findings.claim: Spearman rho=-0.235, p=0.0205, p_adj_BH=0.067, n=97. Small negative cross-sectional association; primarily a between-account structural pattern.

layer1_statistical_rigor.notes: Spearman correct given right-skewed volume. BH-FDR q=0.10 across 6 pairs appropriate for exploratory analysis. 5.5% shared variance honestly characterized as small.

layer3_guardrail_pairing.notes: No domain pairing for ACV defined; cross-sectional structural relationship, not a performance claim.

layer4_domain_plausibility.notes: Larger accounts having lower ACV coverage is plausible. Account medians corroborate (A005 vol=2441/acv=0.79; A004 vol=256/acv=0.87).

simpsons_check.notes: Within-account A001/A002 positive, A003/A004/A005 negative; aggregate is between-account. Upstream restated at correct level — not a hidden reversal.

reviewed_findings.justification: Numbers reproduce exactly. Effect small but real after BH. Upstream's framing as structural-not-actionable is appropriate. Held at B; small effect and within-account inconsistency block grade A.
required_caveats: Primarily between-account structural; within-account direction inconsistent (A001, A002 diverge). Not actionable as a 'lever' — describes portfolio structure.

reviewed_findings.claim: Spearman rho=0.228, Pearson r=0.214, p_adj_BH=0.067, n=100. Small positive association strengthening over the 4-week window (w1 rho=0.03 → w4 rho=0.46).

layer1_statistical_rigor.notes: Spearman primary, Pearson confirmatory (both approximately normal). BH-FDR applied. Within-week n=25 supports descriptive observation only.

layer3_guardrail_pairing.notes: No domain pairing defined; structural cross-sectional finding.

layer4_domain_plausibility.notes: Co-occurrence of higher ACV with higher fill_rate plausible. Upstream correctly flagged A001-exclusion attenuates relationship and temporal strengthening may reflect A001's simultaneous fill decline — confounding disclosed.

simpsons_check.notes: A001/A003/A004 positive within-account, A002 ≈0, A005 slightly negative. Not a reversal; not uniform either. Upstream disclosed.

reviewed_findings.justification: Numbers reproduce exactly including temporal strengthening, Pearson confirmation, A001-sensitivity. Confounding-hypothesis flag is the correct treatment. Grade B correct; A would require uniform within-account confirmation.
required_caveats: Relationship strengthens over the 4-week window and attenuates when A001 is excluded — partial confounding by A001's simultaneous fill_rate decline. Causal interpretation not supported.
May not generalize beyond the current 4-week window.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: Short baseline window caps confidence across all temporal findings
cross_cutting_issues.description: All temporal claims rest on 4 weekly observations. STL, change-point detection, and seasonal adjustment not computable. High R² at n=4 arises trivially. No temporal slope finding can be graded A on this dataset alone.
cross_cutting_issues.recommended_caveat_for_recipient: All trend findings are preliminary indicators pending additional weeks. Re-run when ≥13 cumulative weeks are available before treating any slope as durable.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: No domain context document — guardrail pairings unspecified, severity un-graded
cross_cutting_issues.description: Without domain context, primary/counter pairings cannot be enforced systematically and absolute metric levels cannot be benchmarked against operational targets. Layer-3 returned missing_data for findings without a data-derivable counter-metric. Where pairings could be constructed (TSA-001 paired with A001 volume; RA-003 paired with A003/SKU003 fill_rate), they were performed.
cross_cutting_issues.recommended_caveat_for_recipient: Severity (is this level acceptable?) cannot be assessed without domain targets. Findings describe statistical deviations, not graded business impact.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: Account A001 has multiple simultaneous concerns
cross_cutting_issues.description: A001 fill_rate declining (significant), A001 week-4 volume -12.8% (not significant), and A001's profile partially drives the fill~ACV relationship. Communication Agent should consolidate A001 into a single integrated card.
cross_cutting_issues.recommended_caveat_for_recipient: Render A001 as a single holistic review, not three overlapping items.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: TSA-002 and RA-003 describe the same finding
cross_cutting_issues.description: Both describe A003/SKU003's persistent instock underperformance. RA-003 provides the formal group test (MWU); TSA-002 provides the temporal-persistence characterization. Should render as one consolidated card.
cross_cutting_issues.recommended_caveat_for_recipient: Single action card on A003/SKU003 instock; cite both cross-sectional and temporal evidence inline.

caveats.message: No temporal finding promoted to grade A; all rest on n=4 weekly observations. Highest-confidence findings (A) are the two cross-sectional structural ones (RA-003 entity-level instock gap; RA-004 account-level volume stratification).
Layer-3 guardrail pairing coverage reduced. Functional-domain pairings not specified; pairings constructed from data structure where possible. Severity benchmarking not possible.

TSA-001's reported 95% CI for the A001 fill_rate slope was narrower than independently computed (upstream [-0.0092,-0.0058]; Validator [-0.0112,-0.0038]). Likely t-critical miscalculation at df=2. Finding direction and significance unchanged; the wider CI is authoritative.
Validator independently confirmed 11 rows of prompt-injection content in account_notes. Column correctly dropped at load by upstream agents; zero influence on any computation.

revalidation_summary.notes: TSA-001 classified as partial_match due to CI half-width discrepancy only; point estimate, p-value, R², and SKU-level decomposition reproduce exactly.

lineage.statistics_source: All Validator statistics computed via independent executed Python on smoke-test.csv. account_notes column dropped at load.
